lesson: The C# repo must own reusable authoring, analysis, preset, scoring, and Faust-generation behavior; only the Rust renderer/runtime and experimental compiler branch are intentionally left behind.
source: user direction and migration pass through commit 3246672

lesson: Runtime-facing audio code must avoid unbounded operations: allocation, locks, I/O, console output, OS calls, and context switches do not belong in audio callbacks.
source: PortAudio callback documentation

lesson: Treat patch scripts as deterministic dataflow graphs with explicit rate and block semantics; terse syntax is acceptable only when lowering remains inspectable.
source: Pure Data block/subpatch model and Faust block-diagram model

lesson: SFXR normalized filter values are not linear cutoff frequencies. In Faust export, small SFXR high-pass values must remain very low; mapping hpf directly to thousands of Hz removes the body of pickup, jump, powerup, and blip presets while still leaving them audible enough for weak tests to pass.
source: Aquarium engine native Faust parity test against C# SFXR reference renderer

lesson: BFXR, SFXR, 808, FM, and wobble presets are reference patches for testing and DSL design, not terseness trophies. Built-ins should be readable enough to teach the patch language, and the parser should support multi-line commands instead of forcing all expressive patches into one-line golf.
source: user clarified preset purpose for patch DSL iteration

lesson: The synth reference catalog needs complex examples that combine defaults, templates, multiple voices, modulation buses, FM, formants, noise layers, repeat, and soft clipping. These patches expose DSL readability and composition pressure better than short one-shot SFX presets.
source: user asked for sophisticated patch references

lesson: AquariumSynthCSharp should use external synth and DSP corpora as reference targets, not just internal demo patches. Start with DX7/Dexed SysEx for structured FM, then ZynAddSubFX, Faust examples/libraries, Surge XT, and Cardinal as progressively broader pressure tests. Keep provenance and license state explicit before vendoring fixtures.
source: user clarified any synth can be broken down and rebuilt via Aquarium DSL

lesson: Synth patches must expose expressive runtime parameters. Faust supports this through UI controls and hosted parameter APIs, so Aquarium should compile a graph once and vary declared parameter values without recompilation. Recompile only for structural graph changes.
source: user asked whether Faust can vary one number without recompiling

lesson: AquariumSynthCSharp owns reference-patch correctness. Aquarium should only smoke-test the engine synth host and bundled Faust boundary; SFXR/BFXR/reference parity belongs here so the engine can keep moving while the synth library is under active surgery.
source: user questioned synth tests living in Aquarium

lesson: Aquarium consumes AquariumSynth.Dsl through a pinned package, not a live project reference. Breaking changes in AquariumSynthCSharp are allowed during synth construction as long as a new package version is packed and Aquarium is updated deliberately when the boundary should move.
source: user wanted Aquarium builds insulated from synth-agent work

lesson: Aquarium patch scripts now have first-class `param` declarations and patch/reference model contracts. Faust emission exposes declared parameters as smoothed hslider controls, but target binding is intentionally still absent and warned about; do not claim parameters shape sound until binding is designed.
source: parameter contract implementation on 2026-05-13

lesson: Aquarium parameter binding uses Option B: numeric field values may reference declared parameters with `@/path`, producing exact field bindings such as `/voices/0/filter/lpf`. The field owns the meaning; the parameter owns the runtime value. Avoid target-routing parameter semantics unless a new authority is deliberately mapped.
source: user rejected target binding as a landmine; field-site binding implemented on 2026-05-13

lesson: DX7 reference elaboration starts with structural extraction, not translation. The repo now parses 155-byte voice edit buffers, 128-byte packed voices, and 4104-byte packed 32-voice SysEx banks into neutral DX7 records with checksum validation and ReferencePatch features. Next pressure point is explicit algorithm topology metadata for carriers, modulation edges, and feedback edge location.
source: DX7 SysEx parser slice on 2026-05-13

lesson: DX7 algorithm topology is now derived from the reverse-engineered OPS algorithm ROM table, not hand-drawn diagrams. Topology exposes carrier operators, modulation edges, feedback-register writers, self-feedback operators, delayed-feedback targets, and raw ROM steps. Use this as pressure evidence before adding an Aquarium operator graph layer.
source: DX7 algorithm topology slice on 2026-05-13; Ken Shirriff OPS ROM table

lesson: Two DX7-style Aquarium rebuilds now make the pressure explicit: algorithm 32 maps cleanly to layered additive voices, but algorithm 8 exposes missing graph structure (operator modulation edges, summed modulators, and operator-local self-feedback). Do not patch this with more Voice fields; decide whether an operator graph model owns this invariant.
source: DX7 rebuild catalog slice on 2026-05-13

lesson: DX7 algorithm-8 pressure justified a first-class OperatorGraph abstraction. OperatorGraph owns FM topology: operators, modulation edges, carriers, and feedback. Do not hide this authority inside Voice fields. Remaining DX7 pressure is exact feedback-register timing and rate/level envelopes.
source: operator graph implementation slice on 2026-05-13

lesson: Operator graph authoring should use readable `operator`, `route`, and `carrier` declarations with `env=ad:...` or `env=adsr:...`. Compact `ops=`/`edges=` tuples are acceptable as importer/interchange scaffolding only, not the face of the DSL.
source: user rejected number-soup operator syntax; readable operator graph syntax slice on 2026-05-13

lesson: Aquarium's general envelope model is ADSR: attack, decay, sustain level, release. Note timing belongs to `Note` records: frequency, gate duration, and host/MIDI source. SFXR sustain maps to note gate duration; SFXR punch maps to lower sustain level plus voice gain compensation so ADSR peak remains implicit 1 and the held level sits below the transient.
source: user identified SFXR sustain as note duration and Punch as transient above sustain; punch mapping correction on 2026-05-13

lesson: Prefer Faust-managed MIDI polyphony over Aquarium graph inflation. Aquarium should emit a single voice graph with standard `freq`, `gain`, and `gate` controls plus `[midi:on][nvoices:n]`; Faust architectures own MIDI decoding and voice allocation unless a concrete target proves the boundary insufficient.
source: Faust official MIDI/polyphony docs and user preference for Faust support on 2026-05-13

lesson: Readable operator graph fields bind runtime parameters at exact `/opgraphs/...` field paths: graph gain/note fields, operator ratio/level/feedback/envelope fields, and route index fields. Keep compact `ops=`/`edges=` as importer scaffolding and avoid graph-wide macro routing.
source: operator graph field-site binding slice on 2026-05-13

lesson: DX7 four-rate/four-level operator envelopes can be lowered to labeled Aquarium ADSR approximations plus gate duration, but this is not exact DX7 EG execution. Reference rebuilds should record approximation as matched pressure and exactness as still missing.
source: DX7 envelope approximation slice on 2026-05-13; Yamaha DX7 manual describes EGs as four rates and four levels

lesson: Aquarium Faust output can now be compiled to Faust-generated C# and rendered into mono float samples for AudioAnalyzer comparisons. This proves the candidate side of parity; DX7 parity still requires a real Dexed/reference render or captured licensed fixture, not self-comparison.
source: Faust render parity harness slice on 2026-05-13

lesson: External synth references exist to exercise Aquarium DSL expressiveness, not to clone foreign internals. Consume only references that can be backed by parity tests proving Aquarium can reproduce the behavior in terse, readable syntax; parsing a format is inventory, not evidence.
source: user clarified reference-synth purpose and parity-test requirement on 2026-05-13

lesson: DX7 reference rendering now uses dexed-py as optional test tooling against a public-domain SysEx fixture. Keep fixtures and renderer helpers under the test project, preserve provenance and SHA-256 beside vendored banks, and keep AquariumSynth.Dsl.nupkg free of SysEx/Python/test assets.
source: dexed-py public-domain fixture harness slice on 2026-05-13

lesson: The first thresholded rendered DX7 parity target is analog1.syx voice 13, MC-MM 5-3. Aquarium matches the rendered behavior with a short sine-like patch and explicit score/log-mel/envelope/duration/RMS/zero-crossing/centroid thresholds. Treat this as proof of the parity loop, not proof of exact DX7 operator execution; next target should audibly require operator routing.
source: first thresholded DX7 rendered parity slice on 2026-05-13

lesson: A harder DX7 algorithm-8 target exposed direct operator self-reference as non-renderable Faust. Operator feedback now emits as delayed Faust recursion, which keeps the render path alive and is closer to DX7 feedback pressure; exact DX7 feedback scaling remains unsolved.
source: PRC SYNTH1 parity probe and delayed feedback render fix on 2026-05-13

lesson: DX7 operator output level is nonlinear and includes key/velocity offsets before envelope gain. Added a reference-import helper to approximate effective Aquarium operator levels, but PRC SYNTH1 still only improved to ~0.34 with static levels and ~0.42 with envelope tweaks; hard parity now points at DX7-style operator envelope/gain evolution rather than static level mapping alone.
source: DX7 effective operator level probe on 2026-05-13
